name: ASM Validation Rule Set Template
description: Supplementary validation rules that go BEYOND the official Allotrope JSON schema. The Allotrope schema (validated automatically by the core engine via jsonschema-rs) checks document structure, required fields, and type conformance. These rules add checks the schema does not enforce: value format validation, naming conventions, traceability requirements, and organization-specific rules.
author: AWS ASM Transformation Service

levels.L1: Supplementary schema checks (beyond what jsonschema-rs enforces)
levels.L2: Data quality — handled separately by Data Integrity Verification (field_mapping)

note: The Allotrope JSON schema already validates: $asm.manifest presence, measurement identifier and measurement time as required fields, device type in device control documents, field types, and detector schema conformance. Those checks are NOT duplicated here.

rules.name: Measurement identifiers must be UUID
rules.message: Measurement identifier '{value}' is not a valid UUID format. Schema only checks it's a string — this rule enforces UUID v4.
rules.reference: Allotrope ASM best practice

rules.name: Measurement time must be ISO 8601 with timezone
rules.message: Measurement time '{value}' must be ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Schema only checks it's a string.
rules.reference: Allotrope ASM best practice

rules.name: Valid unit symbols (QUDT)
rules.message: Unit '{value}' is not a recognized QUDT unit symbol. Schema checks unit field exists but not its value.
rules.reference: QUDT ontology

rules.name: Sample identifier required in sample documents
rules.message: Sample document must include a sample identifier. Not always enforced by the schema.
rules.reference: Allotrope ASM best practice

rules.name: Quantity values must have units
rules.message: Measurement value must include a unit field. Safety net for cases where detector schemas may not enforce this.
rules.reference: Allotrope ASM specification — quantity datum pattern

rules.name: Analyst field required
rules.message: The 'analyst' field is required to identify who performed the measurement. Optional in schema but required by building principles.
rules.reference: ASM Building Principles — Instrument Hardcoded Terms

rules.name: Calculated data must have traceability
rules.message: Calculated data must include data source aggregate document for traceability back to source measurements.
rules.reference: ASM Building Principles — Best Practices for Linking Calculated Data

rules.name: Data source must reference measurement identifiers
rules.message: Data source document must include data source identifier referencing a measurement.
rules.reference: ASM Building Principles — Best Practices for Linking Calculated Data

rules.name: Data source must specify feature
rules.message: Data source document must include data source feature identifying which field was used in the calculation.
rules.reference: ASM Building Principles — Best Practices for Linking Calculated Data

rules.name: No hyphenated field names
rules.message: ASM field names should use spaces, not hyphens: '{key}'
rules.reference: ASM Building Principles — ASM Terms and Values Formatting

rules.name: Custom fields must not be empty strings
rules.message: Custom field string values must not be empty. If no value exists, omit the field entirely.
rules.reference: ASM Building Principles — Custom Fields

rules.name: Calculated data must have units
rules.message: Calculated data results must include units. If units are not available, move the field to custom information aggregate document.
rules.reference: ASM Building Principles — Calculated Fields

rules.name: Manufacturer in lowercase
rules.message: Product manufacturer should be written in lowercase: '{value}'
rules.reference: ASM Building Principles — ASM Terms and Values Formatting (Special cases)

rules.name: Device system document required
rules.message: ASM must include a device system document with device identifier and product manufacturer.
rules.reference: ASM Building Principles — Instrument Hardcoded Terms

rules.name: Data system document required
rules.message: ASM must include a data system document with converter name, version, and file information.
rules.reference: ASM Building Principles — Instrument Hardcoded Terms

rules.name: File name in data system document
rules.message: Data system document must include the source file name for traceability.
rules.reference: ASM Building Principles — Source File Formats

rules.name: At least one measurement required
rules.message: ASM must contain at least one measurement document.
rules.reference: ASM Building Principles — Data Hierarchical Structure

rules.name: Device type in device system document
rules.message: Device document must include device type (e.g., 'solution analyzer').
rules.reference: ASM Building Principles — Terms in Multiple Locations

rules.name: UNC path for source traceability
rules.message: Data system document should include UNC path for traceability to the original source file on the file server.
rules.reference: Organization-specific: Source file traceability via mount path
rules.note: CUSTOMIZE: Remove this rule if your organization does not use UNC paths

rules.name: Batch identifier in sample document
rules.message: Sample document should include batch identifier for batch traceability.
rules.reference: Organization-specific: Batch tracking requirement
rules.note: CUSTOMIZE: Remove this rule if batch tracking is not required

rules.name: ASM file identifier format
rules.message: ASM file identifier should follow the pattern: filename-rownumber.json (e.g., MockData1-1.json).
rules.reference: Organization-specific: Output filename convention
rules.note: CUSTOMIZE: Adjust pattern to match your naming convention

rules.name: Data system instance identifier required
rules.message: Data system document should include data system instance identifier (server name).
rules.reference: Organization-specific: Server identification for multi-site deployments
rules.note: CUSTOMIZE: Remove if not applicable to your infrastructure

rules.name: Converter version tracking
rules.message: Data system document should include ASM converter version for change tracking.
rules.reference: Organization-specific: Converter version management
rules.note: CUSTOMIZE: Adjust severity to 'error' if version tracking is mandatory

reference_data.qudt-unit-symbols.json: List of valid QUDT unit symbols. Download from QUDT ontology or use the provided file.
reference_data.allotrope-vocabulary.json: Allotrope Foundation ontology terms. Download from gitlab.com/allotrope-public/asm

how_to_customize: 1. Review all rules and remove any that don't apply to your organization
2. Rules marked with 'note: CUSTOMIZE' are organization-specific — review first
3. Adjust severity levels: 'error' blocks certification, 'warning' is informational
4. Add your own L4 rules for organization-specific requirements
5. Update rule_set_id and author fields to identify your organization
6. Upload the customized rule set via the dashboard or API
customization_guide.adding_rules: Copy an existing rule as a template. Each rule needs: rule_id (unique), name, level, check (type), path (JSON path pattern), severity, and message.
customization_guide.path_syntax: Use ** for recursive descent, [*] for array items, | for alternatives. Example: **.measurement document[*].sample document
